title: gratiola ebracteata
intro: Gratiola ebracteata, also known as American colicroot or blunt-leaved gratiola, is a flowering plant species in the family Plantaginaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and soothing digestive problems. Gratiola ebracteata is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal purposes, and is typically harvested from the roots and leaves of the plant.
